body: Just to add professional backing for this: the Envelope of Function model (used widely in pain rehab) explicitly recommends a 'buffer zone' before the functional limit is reached - warnings at 75% aligns with that framework.
That's exactly the professional grounding we needed. Merging the threshold PR this sprint.

Can confirm this feedback came directly from users in my workload. The 'exceeded' language triggered shame responses in two users who then stopped logging entirely. Behavior change is the whole point - language matters.
Frank - thanks for the professional specifics. We're making this a principle going forward: no shame-adjacent language in any user-facing copy. I'll add it to our content design guidelines.
The retry issue hit me this week - my weekly outcomes script failed midway through a run because of a 503 and lost 45 minutes of work. Tenacity library would be a one-liner fix here.
Fixed in PR #10. The issue was csv.user on Windows doesn't handle \r\n by default unless you open with newline=''. Added a note in the README about this.
This is a great accessibility request. I'll work on it this weekend. The sleep hygiene diagram is the main one - it has 3 figures with no alt text currently.

Prioritizing 'Goals of Care' over other open issues - this is a billing necessity for any rehab using the app for documented care. Without it, the community partner portal can't support professional documentation workflows.
The WCAG 1.4.1 issue is a user safety concern in addition to accessibility - color-blind users may not notice a critical alert at all. Flagging as high priority.
Would love a Spanish PR! The pacing handout source is in /handouts/pacing.md - feel free to fork and PR a pacing-es.md. I can review for professional accuracy with a Spanish-speaking colleague.

Professional rationale for the step order: when you ask about topic before activity baseline, users anchor on their 'sick role' identity and systematically underestimate their actual capacity. I see this in every new chronic pain group evaluation.
For the 30-day view: ideally the graph x-axis ticks would shift automatically - weekly view at 7d, monthly view with 4-week ticks. The data is already there in the API, it's a front-end toggle only.
Added zone II protocols in PR #6. Also included a summary of the 6-week staged mobilization protocol from the ASHT guidelines since that's commonly tested.

The ICD-10 groupings I'd want as filters: M54 (spinal/back pain), M79 (fibromyalgia/other), G89 (central/neuropathic pain), M47 (spondylosis), F45 (somatic symptom), Z87 (personal history). Those cover 90%+ of my chronic pain group workload.
Paper trading only - just learning how indicators work. RSI filter is to avoid buying into overbought momentum. Not financial advice, literally a hobby project.
The custom logo feature is exactly what I needed - Bitterroot Rehab branding is already in my fork. Merged cleanly. Thank you!
The MET table is really well organized. I especially like that you included household tasks (loading dishwasher = 2.0 METs) - those come up constantly in chronic pain group work.
Adding to Q4 sprint. Frank's ICD-10 grouping list is exactly what we need - will build the filter around those 6 categories as the MVP.
Stimulus control worksheet is live in v1.2. Also added a CBT-I session log template and a sleep restriction calculation helper. Let me know if the protocols need adjustment.
Multi-channel sync is more complex than I thought - Amazon and BigCommerce have very different inventory models. Considering a simple read-only dashboard first that shows status per SKU per platform.
